title: q-Askey Deformations of Double-Scaled SYK
authors: Sergio E. Aguilar-Gutierrez, Trivko Kukolj, Josef Seitz
abstract: We construct families of deformations of the double-scaled SYK (DSSYK) model and investigate their bulk interpretation. We introduce microscopic deformations of the SYK model which, after ensemble averaging and in the double-scaling limit, are described by a transfer matrix encoding the recurrence relations of basic orthogonal polynomials in the q-Askey scheme. For certain families of deformations in the semiclassical limit at finite temperature, the chord number (encoding Krylov complexity) corresponds to the length of an Einstein-Rosen bridge connecting an End-Of-The-World brane to an anti-de Sitter asymptotic boundary. By increasing one of the deformation parameters, the models eventually exhibit discrete energy levels, signaling a new geometric transition in sine dilaton gravity. Via the SYK-Schur duality, Krylov complexity also admits a representation-theoretic interpretation as the spread of the SU(2) spin in the index of an $\mathcal{N}=2$ SU(2) gauge theory. We study the operator algebras of the deformed theories. The algebras can be type II$_1$ or type I$_\infty$ factors, depending on the operators that are included. The entanglement entropy between the type II$_1$ algebras for a pure state manifests as an extremal surface through the Ryu-Takayanagi formula. We discuss connections between our results and the emergence of baby universes in the bulk.
summary: This paper constructs a family of deformed SYK models using the q-Askey scheme of orthogonal polynomials and maps them to specific gravitational backgrounds like sine dilaton gravity. It provides a holographic dictionary relating quantum chaos (Krylov complexity) and entanglement to geometric features like Einstein-Rosen bridges and ETW branes.
main_problem: The paper seeks to provide a systematic microscopic construction and bulk holographic interpretation for the full hierarchy of q-Askey deformations of the double-scaled SYK (DSSYK) model.
main_result: The authors demonstrate that these deformations correspond to specific bulk geometries, such as sine dilaton gravity with End-Of-The-World (ETW) branes, and show that Krylov complexity matches the length of Einstein-Rosen bridges in these backgrounds.
method: The study employs the double-scaling limit, ensemble averaging, chord diagrammatic methods, and the theory of basic hypergeometric orthogonal polynomials (the q-Askey scheme) alongside algebraic approaches using von Neumann algebras.
model_or_system: The primary model is the q-Askey deformed Double-Scaled SYK (DSSYK) model, which involves a large number of Majorana fermions with all-to-all interactions and deformations described by the Askey-Wilson scheme.
key_observables: Krylov complexity (spread complexity), entanglement entropy, energy spectrum (continuous and discrete), and the semiclassical partition function.
important_parameters: Deformation parameters (t_i), the q-deformation parameter, the number of fermions (n_F), the double-scaling parameter (lambda), and temperature (beta).
main_assumptions: The analysis relies on the large N limit and the double-scaling limit; it also assumes the validity of the chord diagrammatic expansion for evaluating expectation values.
figures_summary: Figure 16 illustrates the entanglement of ETW branes from both Lorentzian and Euclidean perspectives, showing the distance between the boundary and the brane.
paper_structure: The paper begins with the microscopic construction of the q-Askey deformed SYK Hamiltonian, moves to the derivation of the chord algebra and its relation to orthogonal polynomials, explores the holographic interpretation of Krylov complexity and entanglement entropy, and concludes with the study of the adjoint deformation and its connection to gauge theory.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: Figure 1: Interpretation of the q-Askey deformed theories from different perspectives. (a) Chord-diagrammatic representation, where B(nF ) ⃗t

Figure 2: Example of a chord diagram to evaluate (2.6) with SYK Hamiltonians (black) and SYK operator insertions (orange) as D ( ˆHSYK ˆOSYK)4E DS. Each node in the thermal circle represents a string of Majorana fermions, which are pairwise Wick contracted between each other due to (2.4).

Figure 3: Representation of the two point function (2.32) resulting in a PETS [117]. (a) General case where the inverse temperature βL/R of each subsystem in the boundary is finite. (b) The ETW brane state makes the would-be two-sided geometry corresponding to the TFD, into a one-sided one by taking the βL →∞limit in (2.32). Schematically, this limit pushes it very close to the ETW brane resulting from the operator insertions. While the left system goes to its ground state, the ETW brane remains in a (generalised) q-coherent state [138]. The Euclidean evolution on the right-hand side is generated by the right SYK Hamiltonian ˆH(R) SYK (2.28b) or its deformation ˆH(R) SYK →ˆHnF SYK (2.33)....

Figure 4: (a) Lorentzian Bulk interpretation of the KM state. The ETW brane corresponds to a shockwave propagating outside the black hole (red null rays) generated by inserting heavy operators in the thermal circle (red dot), and we display the minimal length geodesics (blue) joining one asymptotic boundary (black) of an AdS2 black hole to a pair of ETW branes (red). (b) Chord diagram of the KM state, where ˆPs represents a projection on the KM state used in the Hamiltonian moments (2.102).

Figure 5: (a) Semiclassical complexity CS (3.20) and (b) its rate of growth for the HH state of the nF = 0 (orange), 2 (red, t1 = 0.9), 4 (purple, t1 = 0.9, t2 = 0.5), 6 (blue, t1 = 0.9, t2 = 0.5, t3 = 0.3), 8 (black, t1 = 0.9, t2 = 0.5, t3 = 0.3, t4 = 0.5) and the adjoint (brown, χ = 0.5) deformations of the DSSYK model. All cases are displayed for θ = 0.5, and we have rescaled Krylov complexity in units 1/λ, while t is dimensionless since we set J = 1 in (2.4).
